subject: History
question_stem: Who among the following was the founder of the Indian National Congress?
explanation: Allan Octavian Hume (A.O. Hume), a retired British civil servant, was instrumental in founding the Indian National Congress in 1885.

subject: History
question_stem: The 'Quit India' movement was launched in response to which of the following?
explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched in August 1942 in response to the failure of the Cripps Mission.

subject: Polity
question_stem: Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of Right to Life and Personal Liberty. Which of the following in the Constitution of India correctly and appropriately imply the above?
option_a: Article 14 and the provisions under the 42nd Amendment
option_b: Article 17 and the Directive Principles of State Policy
option_c: Article 21 and the freedoms guaranteed in Part III
option_d: Article 24 and the provisions under the 44th Amendment
explanation: The Supreme Court of India in the landmark K.S. Puttaswamy case (2017) unanimously ruled that the Right to Privacy is a fundamental right protected under Article 21.

subject: Polity
question_stem: Which one of the following categories of Fundamental Rights incorporates protection against untouchability as a form of discrimination?
explanation: Article 17 abolishes 'untouchability' and its practice in any form. It falls under the category of 'Right to Equality' (Articles 14-18).

subject: Geography
question_stem: Which one of the following is not a planetary wind?
explanation: Monsoon is a seasonal wind, whereas Trade winds, Westerlies, and Polar easterlies are planetary (permanent) winds.
